title: Barn Door Appliance Cabinet
summary: A plywood cabinet sized to hide a mini fridge and microwave behind a small sliding barn door — a fixed microwave shelf, footer trim, and a face frame with a barn door built to custom hardware.
description:
This cabinet is purpose-built around two specific appliances rather than general storage: a mini fridge on the cabinet floor and a microwave on a fixed shelf above it, both hidden behind one small sliding barn door. **Leave the microwave shelf's exact height undetermined until the fridge is test-fit** — the plan explicitly suggests waiting to add that shelf until after the divider is in place, so the microwave shelf can be positioned perfectly once you know exactly how tall your fridge actually sits inside the cabinet.

Build the plywood carcass with a Kreg jig set for 3/4" material, leaving a consistent clearance gap under the bottom shelf — the divider itself works as a spacer for that gap, the same trick used elsewhere in this catalog for consistent shelf placement. Back supports at the top rear of the cabinet give the whole case something to anchor into behind the scenes, worth adding even though they're invisible in the finished piece.

The face frame can attach either from the outside with visible brad nails and glue, or from the inside with pocket holes for a cleaner look — a real finish-quality choice worth making before you start rather than partway through. **The footer trim pieces are measured and cut to fit your actual assembled cabinet**, not a plan number, since a plywood carcass this size rarely lands on an exact calculated width. Build the door to your specific barn door hardware's requirements; sizing here is deliberately left flexible since hardware kits vary between manufacturers.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Position after test-fitting the fridge in place.
Measure and cut to fit your assembled cabinet.
Measure and cut to fit your assembled cabinet.
Cut 1x6 boards in half to yield 8 top pieces.
Build to fit your barn door hardware.
Build to fit your barn door hardware.

steps.title: Build the carcass
steps.body: Build the carcass using a pocket-hole jig set for 3/4" material, drilling three pocket holes at each joint and attaching with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Use the divider as a spacer to leave a consistent gap under the bottom shelf.

steps.title: Add back supports and the microwave shelf
steps.body: Drill pocket holes on each end of the back supports and attach at the back top of the cabinet. Test-fit the fridge in the lower bay, then position and attach the microwave shelf.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Build the face frame on your workbench with pocket holes, then attach to the front of the cabinet with 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue, or pocket holes from inside the cabinet for a cleaner look.

steps.title: Add the footer
steps.body: Measure and cut the footer side and front pieces to fit your actual assembled cabinet. Nail and glue on.

steps.title: Build the barn door
steps.body: Build the door to fit your specific barn door hardware kit's dimensions, using 1x3 stiles and rails with a plywood back panel.

steps.title: Add the top, hang the door, and finish
steps.body: Attach the top boards to the cabinet. Hang the door with the barn door hardware. Sand and finish.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Small cabinet with a sliding barn door hiding a mini fridge and microwave
